# Claude Code prompt ‚Äî Fix PR #220 (CI failure + renderer findings)

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow
it. PR #220 (`feat/5a2-html-pdf-renderer`, the ¬ß5A.2 HTML‚ÜíPDF renderer) has
auto-merge enabled but its **API CI check is failing**. Push the fixes below to
the **existing `feat/5a2-html-pdf-renderer` branch** so CI re-runs and #220
merges clean. Do **not** open a new PR.

## Confirmed failure (from the CI log)

The `api` job fails at the step `npx puppeteer browsers install chrome`:

```
sh: 1: puppeteer: not found
Error: Process completed with exit code 127.
```

## Root cause

1. pnpm 10 ignores a dependency's build scripts unless it is allow-listed in
   `pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies`. `puppeteer` is **not** allow-listed (the root
   `package.json` `pnpm` block has only `overrides`), so `pnpm install` never
   runs Puppeteer's install script and never downloads Chromium.
2. The compensating CI step `npx puppeteer browsers install chrome` runs from
   the **repo root**, but `puppeteer` is a dependency of `apps/api` ‚Äî its bin
   only exists at `apps/api/node_modules/.bin/puppeteer`, not at the root ‚Äî so
   the `puppeteer` command cannot be resolved ‚Üí exit 127.

## Fix 1 ‚Äî Puppeteer / Chromium provisioning (the CI blocker)

- In the **root `package.json`**, add `puppeteer` to the `pnpm` block:
  ```json
  "pnpm": {
    "onlyBuiltDependencies": ["puppeteer"],
    "overrides": { ...keep all existing overrides... }
  }
  ```
  This lets `pnpm install` run Puppeteer's install script, which downloads the
  correct **pinned** Chrome-for-Testing build for puppeteer 23.11.1.
- In `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, **delete** the line
  `- run: npx puppeteer browsers install chrome` from the `api` job ‚Äî it is now
  redundant and was the failing step.
- Run `pnpm install` locally after the change and **commit any `pnpm-lock.yaml`
  update** so CI's `--frozen-lockfile` install stays consistent.
- Verify the full `api` job goes green ‚Äî **including the 4 Puppeteer
  integration tests** in `pdf-renderer.integration.spec.ts`. If those fail
  because Chromium cannot launch for missing **system libraries** (an
  `error while loading shared libraries` message), add a step to the `api` job
  that installs the Chromium dependency set via `apt-get` before the tests.
  The job is not done until the integration tests actually pass in CI.

## Fix 2 ‚Äî concurrency guard ordering (verification finding 1)

In `apps/api/src/modules/pdf-rendering/pdf-renderer.service.ts`,
`renderHtmlToPdf`: `this.inFlight++` currently runs **after**
`await this.getBrowser()` and `await browser.newPage()` ‚Äî both past the
`inFlight >= MAX_CONCURRENT_RENDERS` check. Concurrent calls therefore all
pass the check before any of them increments, so the cap never engages.
Increment `inFlight` **synchronously, immediately after the check, before any
`await`**; keep the decrement in `finally`. Add a test that fires more than
`MAX_CONCURRENT_RENDERS` concurrent `renderHtmlToPdf` calls and asserts the
limit holds.

## Fix 3 ‚Äî font-load race (verification finding 2)

Same file: `page.setContent(html, { waitUntil: "domcontentloaded" })` resolves
before `@font-face` fonts finish loading, so `page.pdf()` can run before
Outfit / Syne are applied (producing fallback-font output). Change `waitUntil`
to `"load"` (or `"networkidle0"`), and `await page.evaluate(() =>
document.fonts.ready)` after `setContent` and before `page.pdf()`.

## Not in this change

- `interpolate()` does not HTML-escape values (verification finding 3) ‚Äî that
  belongs with the document-migration PRs (¬ß5A.2 PRs 2‚Äì4), which interpolate
  real data. Leave it for now.
- Azure deploy: `onlyBuiltDependencies` also makes the deploy workflow's
  `pnpm install` provision Chromium. The deployed Web App's Chromium **system
  libraries** still need confirming, but that can be verified when ¬ß5A.2 PR 2
  first renders a PDF in a deployed environment ‚Äî not required here.

## Gate

Run the full ¬ß6 pre-PR CI checklist. The `api` job ‚Äî lint, `test:api:serial`
(including the renderer integration tests), and compliance smoke ‚Äî must be
green, as must every other required check. Append a `progress.md` entry noting
PR #220 was fixed-forward (Puppeteer CI provisioning + concurrency/font fixes).
The branch's existing auto-merge will merge #220 once all checks pass ‚Äî do not
open a new PR.
